summary: Fabric v2 grill-me follow-up — engineering landing of grill-locked Q1-Q7 decisions across 10 atomic tasks. Schema additions (fabric-config + api-contracts + event-ledger) lock the rc.2/3/4 protocol surface; cache rename completes v2 vocabulary cleanup; docs split establishes data-vs-API separation; zh-CN dogfood rewrite + bilingual init templates ship the M3 language style end-to-end; final Day-1 gate verifies all dimensions before rc.2 implementation begins. Total budget ~8-12h (~1.5-2 work days). Each task = 1 atomic conventional-commits commit; per-commit Gemini review gates next task.
approach: TASK-001 (doc archive) opens the work. TASK-002/003/004/005 fan out in parallel (no shared files: fabric-config schema / api-contracts schema / event-ledger schema / cache rename). TASK-006 (docs split) merges from 002+003+004 because it documents schema field names. TASK-007 (zh-CN dogfood rewrite) resumes from 006 because docs/data-schema.md is the canonical reference for M3 language policy. TASK-008 (bilingual init templates) resumes from 002 because it consumes knowledge_language config field. TASK-009 (fabric-monorepo own config = zh-CN) resumes from 008 to validate config flow end-to-end. TASK-010 (Day-1 gate) merges from ALL upstream tasks for final verification — pure check, no source change, no commit. Parallel groups: [002, 003, 004, 005] after 001. Sequential tail: 006 → 007 / 008 → 009 → 010.

design_decisions.decision: Q1 — Filename `<id>--<slug>.md`: id prefix + slug suffix (e.g. KT-DEC-0001--boundary-b-async-review.md); layer-flip via `git mv` updating KT↔KP prefix; slug rename = plain `git mv` keeping id stable
design_decisions.rationale: Id-prefix enables sort-by-counter discoverability; slug-suffix preserves human-readable index; double-dash separator avoids collision with regular slug hyphens; git mv is the natural mechanism for both layer-flip and slug-rename without breaking history
design_decisions.tradeoff: Filename is longer than id-only or slug-only — accepted for discoverability

design_decisions.decision: Q2 — Pending stage `pending/<type>/<slug>.md` with late-bind id: counter increments only on approve; pending entries have NO id field in frontmatter; on approve, MCP injects assigned id and `git mv` to canonical path
design_decisions.rationale: Late-bind avoids wasted counter increments on rejected/deferred entries; counter monotonicity preserved (no gaps from rollback); pending stage is treated as drafts not yet entitled to a stable id
design_decisions.tradeoff: Approve action becomes more involved (id assignment + frontmatter mutation + git mv) — accepted because rare event

design_decisions.decision: Q3 — M3 language style: EN headings + zh-CN body + EN tech terms preserved; fabric-config field knowledge_language ('match-existing'|'zh-CN'|'en') default 'match-existing'; fabric-monorepo declares own zh-CN explicitly
design_decisions.rationale: EN headings preserve searchability and prompt-template stability; zh-CN body for non-English-first audiences; tech terms preserved inline because translation degrades precision; match-existing default reduces friction on existing repos
design_decisions.tradeoff: Mixed-language entries may feel inconsistent — accepted as the cost of preserving searchability + audience-fit balance

design_decisions.decision: Q4 — Per-type flat topology + KP fully mirrors KT + independent counters/meta files (KT in repo .fabric/agents.meta.json, KP in ~/.fabric/agents.meta.json) + cache rename rule-test.index → .cache/knowledge-test.index
design_decisions.rationale: Flat layout matches existing dogfood; mirroring keeps personal/team identical for tooling reuse; independent counters avoid cross-layer coupling; .cache/ subdirectory establishes convention for future regenerable artifacts
design_decisions.tradeoff: Two meta files instead of one — MCP merges at read time, accepted for layer isolation

design_decisions.decision: Q5 — .md authoritative, meta derived; revision = sha256(sorted(stable_id+':'+frontmatter_hash)); 2-phase events (promote_started → promoted/promote_failed); explicit git mv for slug rename + layer flip
design_decisions.rationale: Single source of truth (.md) avoids consistency bugs; sorted-input hash gives deterministic revision; 2-phase events enable failure forensics; explicit git mv preserves history
design_decisions.tradeoff: Revision recomputation cost on every meta update — accepted as cheap (sha256 of small input)

design_decisions.decision: Q6 — Existing-MCP adaptation: tags into description_index, layer_filter input + default_layer_filter config, redirect_to error shape post-layer-flip, near_duplicate diagnostic for same-slug-across-layers, ENOENT triggers 1 meta-rebuild retry
design_decisions.rationale: Tags enable filter search rc.3 needs; layer_filter respects user intent (team/personal/both); redirect_to enables stable-link tooling after layer-flip; near_duplicate prevents accidental cross-layer dupes; 1-retry on ENOENT handles transient race conditions
design_decisions.tradeoff: API surface grows by 4 fields + 1 error variant — accepted; alternative (separate APIs per filter mode) much worse

design_decisions.decision: Q7 — Thin MCP / thick Skill principle; fab_extract_knowledge semi-thick input (source_session + recent_paths + user_messages_summary); idempotency key (source_session, type, slug); approve via pending_paths[]; layer-flip via modify.layer (not separate flip action); defer expiry detected by doctor; docs split docs/data-schema.md + docs/mcp-contracts.md
design_decisions.rationale: Thin MCP = deterministic CRUD (testable, idempotent); thick Skill = LLM judgment carries context (taggable, dynamic); semi-thick input lets Skill summarize while MCP persists; pending_paths matches Q2 late-bind; modify.layer reuses existing modify action; doctor handles defer expiry as scheduled lint; docs split clarifies data shape vs API surface
design_decisions.tradeoff: More schema upfront (rc.2/3/4 events + 4 MCP tools all pre-locked here) — accepted because schemas are cheaper to land before consumers exist than after

_metadata.scope_boundary: GRILL-FOLLOWUP ENGINEERING LANDING ONLY — must complete BEFORE rc.2 implementation begins (i.e., before fab_extract_knowledge MCP / fabric-archive skill / Stop hooks)
out_of_scope: fab_extract_knowledge MCP tool implementation (rc.2 — only the Zod schema lands here)
fab_review MCP tool implementation (rc.3 — only the Zod schema lands here)
Full README rewrite + docs/knowledge-types.md + docs/initialization.md + docs/roadmap.md (rc.4)

execution_mode.convergence_review_tool: Agent (Claude Code writes tests, ≥90% new-code coverage)
execution_mode.commit_style: conventional commits (feat/fix/refactor/docs/chore); 1 atomic commit per task; commit message authored autonomously by executor
execution_mode.per_task_gate: After each commit: Gemini reviews diff; issues → Claude Code fixes; pass → next task

_metadata.day_1_gate_source: planning-context.md §Day-1 Gate — 11 bash checks executed by TASK-010 (cache vocabulary + schema additions + docs split + dogfood config + build/test/lint)
convergence_plan_level: All 10 tasks completed with green CI (build + test + lint)

quality_check.method: self-review against planning-context.md and required structure (CLI quality check skipped — ccw spec load returned no planning specs)

validated_dimensions.completeness: All 10 user-specified tasks (001-010) generated as task files; all Q1-Q7 grill decisions covered (Q1 → 007 filename context; Q2 → 003+007 pending; Q3 → 002+007+008+009 language; Q4 → 002+005 topology; Q5 → 004+006 sync; Q6 → 003+006 existing-MCP; Q7 → 003+004+006 new-MCP); Day-1 gate enumerated in 010
validated_dimensions.granularity: Tasks are atomic (1 commit each), 10-120 min each — within target band; TASK-006 (90-120 min docs split) is largest, well-bounded by file scope
validated_dimensions.dependencies: DAG validated: 5-way fan-out (001/002/003/004/005) → 006 merges 002+003+004 → 007 resumes 006 → 008 resumes 002 (independent of 006/007) → 009 resumes 008 → 010 merges ALL; no circular deps; verified via dependency_graph
validated_dimensions.convergence_criteria: All tasks have ≥ 5 quantified, grep/test-verifiable convergence criteria; TASK-010 enumerates 11 deterministic gates
validated_dimensions.implementation_steps: All tasks have ≥ 7 implementation steps with specific commands or actions; complex tasks (006, 008) have ≥ 13 steps
validated_dimensions.constraint_compliance: Aligns with memory constraints (Fabric supported clients = Claude Code/Cursor/Codex CLI; clean-slate preference); no specs/*.md found in `ccw spec load --category planning` — no further spec gate; field naming follows files[].change + convergence.criteria conventions
